description: Add system libgit2 and OpenSSL installation step to CI workflow ci job
steps: Read .github/workflows/ci.yml to understand current job structure
Add 'Install system libraries (libgit2, OpenSSL)' step before cargo build in the ci job: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y libgit2-dev libssl-dev pkg-config
Add LIBGIT2_SYS_USE_PKG_CONFIG: '1' to the workflow-level env block alongside existing env vars (CARGO_INCREMENTAL, CARGO_NET_RETRY, RUST_BACKTRACE)
Verify the install step is positioned after checkout and toolchain setup but before any cargo commands
Run cargo build --workspace locally to confirm no regressions from env var presence

description: Add system libgit2 and OpenSSL installation step to CI workflow coverage job
steps: Read .github/workflows/ci.yml coverage job section
Add the same 'Install system libraries (libgit2, OpenSSL)' step before cargo llvm-cov in the coverage job: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y libgit2-dev libssl-dev pkg-config
Verify LIBGIT2_SYS_USE_PKG_CONFIG env var from workflow-level env block applies to coverage job
Verify the install step is positioned after checkout and toolchain setup but before any cargo commands in the coverage job

description: Replace reqwest with ureq in workspace root Cargo.toml dependency declaration
steps: Read Cargo.toml (workspace root) and locate the [workspace.dependencies] section
Find the reqwest dependency line: reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["blocking", "json", "rustls-tls"] }
Replace it with: ureq = { version = "3", default-features = true }
Add a comment above: # HTTP -- synchronous-only client; no async runtime overhead
Run cargo check --workspace to verify dependency resolution (expect compile error in git.rs until code migration)

description: Update crate-level Cargo.toml files: switch libaipm to ureq, remove reqwest from aipm-pack
steps: Read crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml and locate reqwest = { workspace = true }
Replace reqwest = { workspace = true } with ureq = { workspace = true } in crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml
Read crates/aipm-pack/Cargo.toml and locate reqwest = { workspace = true }
Remove the reqwest = { workspace = true } line entirely from crates/aipm-pack/Cargo.toml (zero source references confirm it is unused)
Run cargo check --workspace to verify dependency changes compile (expect error in git.rs until code migration)

description: Migrate http_get() function from reqwest::blocking to ureq in registry/git.rs
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/registry/git.rs and locate the http_get() function (around lines 152-168)
Remove the reqwest import (use reqwest or similar) if present
Replace the http_get() function body: change reqwest::blocking::get(url) to ureq::get(url).call()
Update the response body reading: replace response.bytes().map(|b| b.to_vec()) with response.into_body().read_to_vec()
Remove the explicit is_success() status check since ureq returns Err for non-2xx responses automatically
Update the doc comment from 'reqwest::blocking' to 'ureq'
Preserve the existing Error::Io mapping pattern with format! strings
Run cargo build --workspace to verify the migration compiles
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint warnings

description: Run full build, test, clippy, fmt, and coverage gate to verify all changes pass quality checks
steps: Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint warnings
Run cargo build --workspace to verify clean compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' and verify >= 89% branch coverage
Fix any coverage gaps by adding additional unit tests for uncovered branches
